sources.notes: Count value intervals [l,r] such that the induced 2 by n binary grid has a down-right path of ones.
Official editorial proves minimal feasible r_l is monotone in l and maintains deactivated cells in both rows.

annotation.summary: Count all value ranges [l,r] for which cells with values inside the range contain a valid down-right path in a 2-row grid.
annotation.constraints: There are 2n possible values, so O(n^2) range checks are too slow; a monotone two-pointer sweep is intended.
annotation.core_idea: For fixed l, if [l,r] is good then any larger r is also good, and the minimum good r_l is nondecreasing as l increases. Sweep l and r with two pointers while activating/deactivating cells by value. In a 2-row grid, a path exists iff the first inactive top-row column lies strictly after the last inactive bottom-row column plus one, with endpoints active.
annotation.complexity: O(n log n) per test case using ordered sets.
tricks: activate values by sliding value range

solution_variants.name: value-range-two-pointers
solution_variants.summary: Maintain active cells for the current value interval and use ordered sets of inactive cells to test path existence.

tags.evidence: The answer is counted by sweeping l and monotonically increasing the minimal feasible r.
The path check can be supported by ordered sets or segment-tree-like min/max active position structures.
After finding each minimal r_l, the count adds all larger endpoints for that l.
